title: How Necrosis 'Tracks' Up The Vasculature
excerpt: If you block a super labial artery you get necrosis that follows up the lip and often even leads right to the tip of the nose so tell us a bit more about this roundness like how would we differentiate it from what we've been used to seeing well it's really to do with the fact tha…

segments.text: if you block a super labial artery you get necrosis that follows up the lip and often even leads right to the tip of the nose so tell us a bit more about this roundness like how would we differentiate it from what we've been used to seeing well it's really to do with the fact that if you block a blood vessel they are literally like tree trunks they're like th they branch out and you tend to see it following a line of where the vessel is so if you block the superchocular artery you often get an area of necrosis that follows that artery into the forehead you can see it if you block a super labial artery you get necrosis that follows up the lip and often even leads right to the tip of the nose these are all indications that it's following the vasculature and blocking the capillaries that lead from that vasculature tracking yeah along the vessel so if you know your anatomy it becomes really clear you can see it in lips i've certainly seen an inferior labial artery occlusion that you could see tracking down heading back towards the facial artery so you could see the area on the lip and then

transcript: if you block a super labial artery you get necrosis that follows up the lip and often even leads right to the tip of the nose so tell us a bit more about this roundness like how would we differentiate it from what we've been used to seeing well it's really to do with the fact that if you block a blood vessel they are literally like tree trunks they're like th they branch out and you tend to see it following a line of where the vessel is so if you block the superchocular artery you often get an area of necrosis that follows that artery into the forehead you can see it if you block a super labial artery you get necrosis that follows up the lip and often even leads right to the tip of the nose these are all indications that it's following the vasculature and blocking the capillaries that lead from that vasculature tracking yeah along the vessel so if you know your anatomy it becomes really clear you can see it in lips i've certainly seen an inferior labial artery occlusion that you could see tracking down heading back towards the facial artery so you could see the area on the lip and then the pellet that followed it
